name: Environmental Effects and Hazards
description: Rules for environmental effects that can affect characters during adventures, including extreme conditions, terrain hazards, and weather effects

overview.title: Environmental Effects
overview.description: Characters crossing a frigid tundra might suffer the effects of extreme cold, while a visit to a cloud giant's castle might subject characters to the effects of high altitude
overview.note: The following sections provide rules for handling these and other environmental effects

deep_water.title: Deep Water
deep_water.description: Swimming through deep water presents additional challenges

mechanics.frequency: After each hour of swimming in deep water
mechanics.requirement: A creature that lacks a Swim Speed

extreme_cold.title: Extreme Cold
extreme_cold.description: When the temperature is 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower
extreme_cold.requirement: Creature exposed to the extreme cold

mechanics.frequency: At the end of each hour

resistances: Creatures that have Resistance or Immunity to Cold damage automatically succeed on the save

extreme_heat.title: Extreme Heat
extreme_heat.description: When the temperature is 100 degrees Fahrenheit or higher
extreme_heat.requirement: Creature exposed to the extreme heat and without access to drinkable water

mechanics.frequency: At the end of each hour
dc_progression: Increases by 1 for each additional hour

disadvantage: Creatures wearing Medium or Heavy armor have Disadvantage on the save
resistances: Creatures that have Resistance or Immunity to Fire damage automatically succeed on the save

frigid_water.title: Frigid Water
frigid_water.description: A creature can be immersed in frigid water for a limited time before suffering ill effects

mechanics.safe_duration: A number of minutes equal to the creature's Constitution score before suffering any ill effects
mechanics.additional_minutes: Each additional minute spent in frigid water requires the creature to succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or gain 1 Exhaustion level

resistances: Creatures with Resistance or Immunity to Cold damage automatically succeed on the save
Creatures that are naturally adapted to living in ice-cold water automatically succeed on the save

heavy_precipitation.title: Heavy Precipitation
heavy_precipitation.description: Heavy rain or heavy snowfall conditions
effects: Everything within the area is Lightly Obscured
Creatures in the area have Disadvantage on all Wisdom (Perception) checks
heavy_precipitation.additional_effects_rain: Heavy rain also extinguishes open flames

high_altitude.title: High Altitude
high_altitude.description: Traveling at altitudes of 10,000 feet or higher above sea level
high_altitude.cause: Reduced amount of oxygen in the air

mechanics.travel_pace: Each hour such a creature spends traveling at high altitude counts as 2 hours for the purpose of determining how long that creature can travel

acclimation.requirement: Creatures can become acclimated to a high altitude by spending 30 days or more at this elevation
acclimation.limitation: Creatures can't become acclimated to elevations above 20,000 feet unless they are native to such environments

mechanics.trigger: A creature that moves onto slippery ice for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there

strong_wind.title: Strong Wind
strong_wind.description: High winds that create dangerous conditions

effects.effect: Imposes Disadvantage on ranged attack rolls with weapons
A flying creature in a strong wind must land at the end of its turn or fall

strong_wind.desert_variant: A strong wind in a desert can create a sandstorm that imposes Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks

thin_ice.title: Thin Ice
thin_ice.description: Fragile ice that may break under weight

mechanics.trigger: Whenever the total weight on an area of thin ice exceeds its tolerance
mechanics.consequence: The ice in that area breaks
mechanics.effect: All creatures on broken ice fall through

thin_ice.aftermath: Below the ice is frigid water (see Frigid Water above)
